description: Testing video upload functionality including file sources, formats, naming conventions, size limitations and error handling

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Drag drop video / Click Upload button to upload video from below 
+ PC
+ Shared device: Mobile ...
+ USB, Facebook, Instagram..
expected_result:
+ Can upload video successfully
+ Update into DB correctly
+ Video displays in published WEB/APP correctly as setting
+ Video displays in published WEB/APP correctly when EDIT uploaded video

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Upload video with below extension
+ mp4, avi ....
expected_result:
+ Can upload video successfully
+ Update into DB correctly
+ Video displays in published WEB/APP correctly as setting
+ Video displays in published WEB/APP correctly when EDIT uploaded Video
+ Video displays in published WEB/APP correctly when add new Video with new extension

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Upload Video with incorrect data
+ Incorrect size: + 0 KB, > Max size, < Min size
+ Incorrect extension: jpg, png, txt, xls...
+ XLS file that has been changed extention from video file
+ Video file that has been changed extention from xls file
expected_result:
+ Show error message properly
+ Cannot upload image
+ Not update into DB

test_view_points.viewpoint: Upload 2 videos with the same name
expected_result:
+ Show error message properly [Base on spec]
+ Or auto change name of file into File 1, File 2 [Base on spec]

test_view_points.viewpoint: Upload multi videos at the same time
expected_result: Can upload with the max number of files as spec

test_view_points.viewpoint: Click Cancel when uploading Video
expected_result:
+ Uploading process is stopped properly
+ There is no broken file displaying in upload destination

test_view_points.viewpoint: After uploading file, delete the origin file
expected_result: Uploaded file not be deleted

item_type: Check content of video after uploading
description: Testing video playback functionality including source verification, thumbnail display, and default playback behavior

test_view_points.viewpoint: Check source of video
expected_result:
Video is embeded with correct source in Spec
Ex:  Youtube, vimeo,

test_view_points.viewpoint: Check thumbnail of video
expected_result:
1. Thumbnail is correct as spec
2. The size of thumbnail is correct
3. When hover on video, thumbnail display is correct as spec

test_view_points.viewpoint: Check default timeline
expected_result: Show elapsed timing HH:MM:SS / HH:MM:SS correctly

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Check default running
+ When open a website containing video
+ When open a website containing video and scroll to the area of video
+ When open a website containing video list
expected_result:
1. Specified video is auto-run / Pause [Base on spec]
2. Other videos are pause

item_type:
Check logic running a video (embed in website
Ex: StarMovie, BOX...) in PC/Mobile
description: Testing comprehensive video playback functionality including controls, toolbar behavior, progress tracking, and multi-screen compatibility

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Check running video from
+ Run video from the start
+ Run video from the middle
expected_result:
+ Video is displayed correctly
+ In browser, Volumn icon is displayed correctly as video is running

test_view_points.viewpoint:
+ Check NEXT / PREVIOUS / PAUSE button
+ Check Run - Pause - Run - Pause - Run continuously
+ Check Drag timing bar to go to next/previous screen
expected_result: Can go to next/previous screen in all circumstances

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Check tool bar when
+ When video is playing
+ When tool bar doesn not display, hover on video to show tool bar, then hover out video
expected_result:
+ When video is playing, tool bar is hidden after some seconds
+ When hover on => hover out video, tool bar displays then auto hide after some seconds

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Check progress bar
+ Loaded progress: Color
+ Unloaded progress: Color
expected_result: Show color of loaded progress/unloaded progress properly as spec

item_type: Check when video run to the end, it will rerun when set loop function
description: Testing video loop functionality and automatic loading of new videos

test_view_points.viewpoint:
+ Set Loop function
+ Run a video to the end
expected_result: Video will run again from start

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Check automatically loading new video when opening video is going to the end
+ Video is opening with small screen. When video is nearly going to the end, change the view screen to normal screen
+ Video is opening with small screen. When video is nearly going to the end, change the view screen to full screen
+ Video is opening with normal screen. When video is nearly going to the end, change the view screen to small screen
+ Video is opening with normal screen. When video is nearly going to the end, change the view screen to full screen
+ Video is opening with full screen. When video is nearly going to the end, change the view screen to small screen
+ Video is opening with full screen. When video is nearly going to the end, change the view screen to normal screen
expected_result:
1. Can go to next video successfully
2. Loading icon is displayed properly and icon suitable with the type of screen
3. Setting panel is displayed properly and suitable with type of screen

item_type: Check status of video when video is stopped unexpectedly
description: Testing video behavior during system interruptions and recovery

test_view_points.viewpoint:
When video is running, do below action 
+ Lock PC 
+ Log off PC 
+ Restart PC / Shutdown PC
Login PC again
expected_result:
+ After lock PC, when login, Video continues running
+ After log off/ restart/ shutdown, Video browser is closed correctly. Can open video again properly

item_type: Check open a new video while a video is being openned
description: Testing video switching behavior across different view modes

test_view_points.viewpoint:
+ Video is opening with small screen (default mode). Run video and change the view screen to normal screen. Go to video list and click to open another video
+ Video is opening with small screen (default mode). Run video and change the view screen to full screen. Go to video list and click to open another video
+ Video is opening with medium screen (theatre mode). Run video and change the view screen to small screen. Go to video list and click to open another video
+ Video is opening with medium screen (theatre mode). Run video and change the view screen to full screen. Go to video list and click to open another video
expected_result:
1. Can go to new video successfully
2. Loading icon is displayed properly and icon suitable with the type of screen
3. Setting panel is displayed properly and suitable with type of screen

item_type: Check auto-scroll to top when open video at the middle/bottom of the list
description: Testing auto-scroll behavior when opening videos from different list positions

test_view_points.viewpoint:
+ Run video for some minutes
+ Go to video list and open a video that is listing at the middle of screen / at the end of screen
expected_result: It will auto scroll to top and show video at the top of screen

description: Testing volume control functionality including different levels, persistence across video changes, system events, and playback controls

test_view_points.viewpoint: Set volumn with normal level
expected_result: Can hear the voice with normal level

test_view_points.viewpoint: Set volumn with mute
expected_result: Cannot hear the voice

test_view_points.viewpoint: Set volumn with max level
expected_result: Can hear the voice with max level

test_view_points.viewpoint: Change volumn continuously
expected_result: The voice hearing is changed continuously

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Set volumn with normal level/mute/max level
Run video to the end to auto-load to new video
expected_result: Volumn setting is unchanged when open to new video

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Set volumn with normal level/mute/max level
Not pause the opening video
Lock the PC/Screen lock mobile
Then login PC again
expected_result: Volumn of video is unchanged after lock and login again

test_view_points.viewpoint: Check volumn when click Play - Pause - Previous - Next - Drag to new slides in timing bar
expected_result: Volumn  of video is unchanged when click Play - Pause - Previous - Next - Drag to new slides

description: Testing subtitle toggle functionality and its persistence across different video playback scenarios and system events

test_view_points.viewpoint: Set subtitle display = ON
expected_result: Subtitle is displayed properly

test_view_points.viewpoint: Set subtitle display = OFF
expected_result: Subtitle is not displayed

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Set subtitle ON/OFF
Run video to the end to auto-load to new video
expected_result: Subtitle setting is unchanged when open to new video

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Set subtitle ON/OFF
Not pause the opening video
Lock the PC/Screen lock mobile
Then login PC again
expected_result: Subtitle of video is unchanged after lock and login again

test_view_points.viewpoint: Check subtitle when click Play - Pause - Previous - Next - Drag to new slides in timing bar
expected_result: Subtitle of video is unchanged when click Play - Pause - Previous - Next - Drag to new slides

description: Testing video playback speed controls and persistence across different playback scenarios and system events

test_view_points.viewpoint: Set Speed = Normal/Slow/Fast
expected_result: Video is displayed correctly at that speed

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Set Speed = Normal/Slow/Fast
Run video to the end to auto-load to new video
expected_result: Speed setting is unchanged when open to new video

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Set Speed = Normal/Slow/Fast
Not pause the opening video
Open another video
expected_result: Speed setting is unchanged when open to new video

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Set Speed = Normal/Slow/Fast
Not pause the opening video
Lock the PC/Screen lock mobile
Then login PC again
expected_result: Speed of video is unchanged after lock and login again

test_view_points.viewpoint: Check speed when click Play - Pause - Previous - Next - Drag to new slides in timing bar
expected_result: Speed of video is unchanged when click Play - Pause - Previous - Next - Drag to new slides

description: Testing video quality settings including manual selection, auto-quality based on internet speed, and persistence across different scenarios and system events

test_view_points.viewpoint: Set quality with optional quality
expected_result: Video is displayed correctly at that quality

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Set quality with optional quality
Run video to the end to auto-load to new video
expected_result:
Quality list of new video is displayed properly (each video has different Quality option)
New video is set with correct Quality as spec

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Set Quality with optional quality
Not pause the opening video
Lock the PC/Screen lock mobile
Then login PC again
expected_result: Quality of video is unchanged after lock and login again

test_view_points.viewpoint: Check quality when click Play - Pause - Previous - Next - Drag to new slides in timing bar
expected_result: Quality of video is unchanged when click Play - Pause - Previous - Next - Drag to new slides

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Check quality of video when select "Auto" in Quality with below internet Speed

1. Internet speed in PC = 1Mbps
2. Internet speed in PC = 2Mbps
...
expected_result:
Confirm that video is played with suitable quality as spec 

EX:
1. Internet speed in PC = 1Mbps ==> Video will be run with quality = 720p. In quality it will show as "Auto 720p" ( Base on requirement)
2. Internet speed in PC = 2Mbps ==> Video will be run with quality = 1080p. In quality it will show as "Auto 1080p" ( Base on requirement)

item_type: Some special case when Run video
description: Testing video playback behavior under various interruptions and special conditions including advertisements, system events, connectivity issues, and file operations

test_view_points.viewpoint: When opening video, an advertising is displayed
expected_result:
Can view advertising
Can skip that advertising
Can continue with the video

test_view_points.viewpoint: When opening video, there is a call/a message/a notification/an automatic virus can run...
expected_result:
Error is not displayed
Can get call/message/notification/virus scan correctly
Can continue view video properly

test_view_points.viewpoint:
When opening video, internet is disconnect
Then connect internet again
expected_result: After connect internet again, can continue view video properly

test_view_points.viewpoint: When opening video, that video is deleted from mobile/pc
expected_result:
1. If spec is to accept delete video when video is opening
Can delete video
Can continue to view video

2. If spec is not accept to delete video when video is opening
Show warning message and cannot delete video
Can continue to view video

test_view_points.viewpoint: When opening video, move that video to another location in mobile/pc
expected_result:
1. If spec is to accept cut video when video is opening
Can cut video
Can continue to view video

2. If spec is not accept to cut video when video is opening
Show warning message and cannot delete video
Can continue to view video

test_view_points.viewpoint:
Open video in mobile
Then copy that video from SDcard to PC
expected_result: Error is not displayed when copying
